message.content:
Edit the file `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/lenses/RBS-Specification.adoc` to introduce a new `at_operator` architectural term and replace specific `{rbtr_consumer}` references where "Consumer Role" is being misused to mean "the person at the keyboard."

**Context:** The Consumer Role is one of several roles (Consumer, Governor, Director, Retriever, Payor). The person who sits at the workstation and assumes these roles is a distinct concept — the "Operator." Currently many definitions incorrectly say "Consumer Role" when they mean "the person." We're fixing that.

**Edits to make (read the file first, then apply these):**

1. **Add mapping entries** — near the existing `rbtr_consumer` mappings (around lines 68-70), add these new lines right after them:
```
:at_operator:                <<operator,Operator>>
:at_operator_p:              <<operator,Operator's>>
:at_operator_s:              <<operator,Operators>>
```

2. **Fix abstract** (around line 180) — change:
"at the\n{rbtr_consumer}\n{at_workstation}." → "at the\n{at_operator_p}\n{at_workstation}."

3. **Fix `at_workstation` definition** (around line 1100-1102) — change:
"where the\n{rbtr_consumer}\nruns the" → "where the\n{at_operator}\nruns the"

4. **Fix `at_user_repo` definition** (around lines 1310-1320) — there are three `{rbtr_consumer}` references:
   - Line ~1310: "owned by a\n{rbtr_consumer}." → "owned by an\n{at_operator}."
   - Line ~1312: "The\n{rbtr_consumer}\nchooses" → "The\n{at_operator}\nchooses"
   - Line ~1317: "The\n{rbtr_consumer}\nconfigures" → "The\n{at_operator}\nconfigures"

5. **Refine `rbtr_consumer` definition** (around lines 1342-1351) — change the full definition text from:
```
{rbtr_consumer}::
An individual operating the
{at_rbm_system}
to run containerized services securely on their
{at_workstation}.
{rbtr_consumer_s}
control service configuration through
{at_nameplate_s}
and interact with services through controlled interfaces, distinct from infrastructure operators who build and deploy the system.
```
To:
```
{rbtr_consumer}::
The
{rbtr_role}
an
{at_operator}
assumes to run containerized services securely on their
{at_workstation}.
{rbtr_consumer_s}
control service configuration through
{at_nameplate_s}
and interact with services through controlled interfaces, distinct from roles that build and deploy the system.
```

6. **Fix `cfg_station_regime` definition** (around line 1385) — change:
"Each\n{rbtr_consumer}\nmaintains" → "Each\n{at_operator}\nmaintains"

7. **Fix `cmk_external_rule` definition** (around line 1624) — change:
"for\n{rbtr_consumer}\nuse" → "for\n{at_operator}\nuse"

8. **Fix `st_image_store` definition** (around line 1666) — change:
"{rbtr_consumer_p}\n{at_workstation}" → "{at_operator_p}\n{at_workstation}"

9. **Add Operator definition** — place it right before the `[[consumer]]` definition (before line ~1342). Add:
```
[[operator]]
{at_operator}::
An individual who maintains a local installation of the
{at_rbm_system}
on their
{at_workstation}
and holds RBRA Files that enable assumption of the system's defined roles.

```

IMPORTANT: Read the file first before making edits. Be precise with the Edit tool — match exact strings including newlines. Do NOT change any lines I haven't listed above.

   175→[abstract]
   176→.Abstract
   177→The Recipe Bottle Makefile Service enables safe and effective use of foreign
   178→{st_container_s}
   179→at the
   180→{rbtr_consumer}
   181→{at_workstation}.
   182→
   183→== System Overview 
   184→
   185→=== Core Trust Challenge
   186→
   187→Modern development increasingly requires running foreign code with access to local work.
   188→
   189→{st_container}-
   190→based development tools processing source code, local AI/ML tools analyzing proprietary data, and third-party services needing controlled network access create fundamental security challenges.
   191→
   192→This creates a fundamental trust dilemma:
   193→
   194→* {st_container_s}
   195→provide powerful tool isolation
   196→* But they need controlled access
   197→to do useful work  
   198→* Standard
   199→{st_container}
   200→networking
   201→grants unfettered outbound access or else network services completely unavailable.
   202→
   203→=== The Bottle Pattern
   204→
   205→{at_rbm_system}
   206→solves this by creating two simple, robust boundaries around a certain
   207→{st_container}
   208→type here referred to as a
   209→{at_bottle_container}:
   210→
   211→. File Access Boundary
   212→* {at_bottle_container}
   213→sees only explicitly controlled set of
   214→{st_volume_mount_s}
   215→* No other access
   216→to
   217→{at_workstation}
   218→filesystem
   219→
   220→. Network Boundary  
   221→* {at_bottle_container}
   222→has no direct network access
   223→* All traffic
   224→flows through dedicated
   225→{at_sentry_container},
   226→a
   227→{st_container}
   228→configured with very tight network access and visibility controls.
   229→* Only declared
   230→{st_port_map_s}
   231→and destinations
   232→allowed
   233→* Full logging
   234→of allowed and denied traffic
   235→
   236→Each
   237→{at_bottle_service}
   238→maintains these boundaries through a three-container architecture:
   239→
   240→* One
   241→{at_sentry_container}
   242→enforcing network policy on dual networks (bridge and enclave)
   243→* One
   244→{at_censer_container}
   245→establishing and configuring the network namespace
   246→* One
   247→{at_bottle_container}
   248→running the actual service, sharing the censer's network namespace
   249→* Clear configuration
   250→in a single
   251→{at_nameplate}
   252→file
   253→
   254→The
   255→{at_censer_container}
   256→is a privileged container that establishes the network namespace, configures routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
   257→{at_sentry_container},
   258→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
   259→{at_bottle_container}
   260→via `--net=container:censer`.
   261→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet, and the
   262→{at_bottle_container}
   263→experiences only a functional path to its
   264→{at_sentry_container}
   265→gateway
   266→
   267→=== Security Properties
   268→
   269→The
   270→{at_rbm_system}
   271→maintains following essential guarantees:
   272→
   273→. File Access Control
   274→* {at_bottle_container_s}
   275→can access only explicitly configured
   276→{st_volume_mount_s}
   277→* No access to broader system resources
   278→
   279→. Network Isolation 
   280→* All traffic flows through dedicated
   281→{at_sentry_container}
   282→* Each permitted traffic flow maintains exactly one controlled pathway
   283→* Precise ingress, forwarding, and NAT rules enforce strict boundaries
   284→* Only explicitly configured
   285→{st_port_map_s}
   286→allow traffic between
   287→{at_transit_network} and {at_enclave_network}
   288→* {at_bottle_container_s}
   289→have no direct network access; all connectivity flows through
   290→{at_sentry_container_s}
   291→* Comprehensive traffic logging
   292→
   293→. Service Separation
   294→* Each
   295→{at_bottle_service}
   296→fully isolated
   297→* No cross-service communication
   298→* Independent security policies
   299→* Clean state separation
   300→
   301→// TODO fixup this language realized missing during pod namespace try
   302→. Service Access Control
   303→* External access to services only permitted when {rbrn_entry_mode} is enabled
   304→* No inter-container communication allowed within pods
   305→* All service access must originate from outside the pod
   306→* Entry traffic flows: external → pod →
   307→{at_sentry_container} → {at_bottle_container}
   308→
   309→These properties hold across all operations and failure modes.
   310→
   311→== {opss_sentry_start}
   312→
   313→The
   314→{at_rbm_console}
   315→defines the
   316→{opss_sentry_start},
   317→a
   318→{cmk_external_rule}
   319→which is the precisely ordered sequence of
   320→{cmk_recipe_line_s}
   321→needed to start a
   322→{at_sentry_container}
   323→that enforces network policy for a new
   324→{at_bottle_service}.
   325→
   326→=== {mkr_network_create}
   327→
   328→This sequence of
   329→{cmk_recipe_line_s}
   330→within the
   331→{opss_sentry_start}
   332→establishes required networks for the
   333→{at_bottle_service}:
   334→
   335→. Remove Existing
   336→{at_transit_network}
   337→* Remove the
   338→{at_transit_network}
   339→named
   340→{at_transit_network_name},
   341→ignoring errors
   342→* This ensures a clean state before creating the new transit network
   343→
   344→. Create New
   345→{at_transit_network}
   346→* Use standard Podman network primitives to create a bridge network named
   347→{at_transit_network_name}
   348→* The IP address range or any other attributes remain consistent with the
   349→{rbrn_moniker}-uplink
   350→definition
   351→* IPv6 remains disabled, and external connectivity is enabled
   352→
   353→. Remove Existing
   354→{at_enclave_network}
   355→* Remove the
   356→{at_enclave_network}
   357→named
   358→{at_enclave_network_name},
   359→ignoring errors
   360→* This ensures a clean state before creating the new enclave network
   361→
   362→. Create New
   363→{at_enclave_network}
   364→* Use standard Podman network primitives to create an isolated bridge network named
   365→{at_enclave_network_name}
   366→* Configure the network with subnet
   367→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
   368→* Disable external connectivity to ensure isolation
   369→* Allow netavark to manage IP address assignment for containers on this network
   370→
   371→After this sequence completes, both networks exist in standard Podman configuration. The
   372→{at_sentry_container}
   373→will connect to both networks, while the
   374→{at_bottle_container}
   375→will connect only to the
   376→{at_enclave_network}.
   377→Before the
   378→{at_bottle_container}
   379→starts, an
   380→{at_ebpf_program}
   381→will be attached to its veth interface to ensure all traffic routes through the
   382→{at_sentry_container}.
   383→
   384→=== {mkr_network_connect}
   385→
   386→This sequence of
   387→{cmk_recipe_line_s}
   388→within the
   389→{opss_sentry_start}
   390→establishes the secondary network interface connected to
   391→{at_enclave_network}:
   392→
   393→. Connect Container to Enclave Network
   394→* Attach the
   395→{at_sentry_container}
   396→to the
   397→{at_enclave_network}
   398→using standard Podman network connect
   399→* Verify successful network attachment
   400→* Validate interface naming sequence - eth0 must be the transit network interface
   401→* The enclave network interface must appear as eth1
   402→
   403→. Verify Network Interface Configuration
   404→* Execute within sentry container context
   405→* {mkc_interface_check}
   406→with interface `eth1` and timeout_s `5`
   407→** Exit on first success
   408→** Fail if timeout reached
   409→
   410→. Configure Sentry IP Address
   411→* Remove the auto-assigned IP address from eth1
   412→* Assign the permanent
   413→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
   414→address to eth1
   415→* This address will serve as the gateway for the
   416→{at_bottle_container}
   417→after eBPF frame rewriting
   418→
   419→After this sequence completes, the
   420→{at_sentry_container}
   421→has connectivity on both networks with the correct IP configuration, ready to receive traffic from the
   422→{at_bottle_container}
   423→once the
   424→{at_ebpf_program}
   425→is attached.

   427→=== {scr_security_config}
   428→
   429→One unified script executes these
   430→{cmk_script_line_s}
   431→in sequence, halting early if any
   432→{cmk_script_line}
   433→has a nonzero unix status:
   434→
   435→1. {scr_iptables_init} 
   436→2. {scr_port_setup}
   437→3. {scr_access_setup}
   438→4. {scr_dns_step}
   439→
   440→==== Phase 1: {scr_iptables_init} 
   441→
   442→Establishes foundational security state:
   443→
   444→. Base Chain Configuration:
   445→* Flush all rules from filter and nat tables
   446→* Set policy DROP on all base chains (INPUT, FORWARD, OUTPUT)
   447→* Accept loopback interface traffic unconditionally via first rule in INPUT chain
   448→
   449→. State Tracking Setup:
   450→* Insert RELATED,ESTABLISHED tracking as second rule in chains:
   451→** INPUT chain with "-m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT"
   452→** FORWARD chain with same state match
   453→** OUTPUT chain with same state match
   454→
   455→. RBM Chain Creation:
   456→* Create in filter table with default policy DROP:
   457→** RBM-INGRESS for input control
   458→** RBM-EGRESS for output control
   459→** RBM-FORWARD for forwarding control
   460→* Insert jumps as third rule in respective base chains:
   461→** INPUT jumps to RBM-INGRESS
   462→** OUTPUT jumps to RBM-EGRESS
   463→** FORWARD jumps to RBM-FORWARD
   464→
   465→==== Phase 2: {scr_port_setup}
   466→
   467→If
   468→{rbrn_entry_mode}
   469→is disabled, this sequence is skipped.
   470→If
   471→{rbrn_entry_mode}
   472→is enabled, this sequence configures service port mapping:
   473→
   474→. Socat Proxy Configuration:
   475→* Launch socat proxy process in background:
   476→** Listen on TCP port
   477→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}
   478→** Forward connections to
   479→{at_bottle_container}
   480→IP (
   481→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
   482→) on port
   483→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
   484→** Enable connection forking and address reuse
   485→** Log output to `/var/log/socat-proxy.log`
   486→* Verify socat process started successfully
   487→
   488→. Filter Configuration:
   489→* Add rule to RBM-INGRESS accepting incoming traffic from bridge network:
   490→** Interface eth0
   491→** Protocol TCP
   492→** Destination port
   493→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}
   494→* Add rule to RBM-EGRESS accepting outbound connections to bottle:
   495→** Interface eth1
   496→** Protocol TCP
   497→** Destination
   498→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
   499→** Destination port
   500→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
   501→* Add rule to RBM-INGRESS accepting return traffic from bottle:
   502→** Interface eth1
   503→** Protocol TCP
   504→** Source
   505→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
   506→** Source port
   507→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
   508→
   509→==== Phase 3: {scr_access_setup}
   510→
   511→If
   512→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   513→is disabled, this sequence:
   514→
   515→* Verifies DROP policy exists for all filter table chains
   516→* Removes NAT rules except those marked "RBM-PORT-FORWARD"
   517→* Adds explicit DROP rules for non-port traffic:
   518→** RBM-EGRESS: DROP all eth0 output
   519→** RBM-FORWARD: DROP all eth1 forward
   520→
   521→If
   522→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   523→is global or allowlist, this sequence:
   524→
   525→. Network Interface Configuration:
   526→* Enable IPv4 forwarding
   527→* Disable IPv6
   528→* Set rp_filter to strict mode (1) on all interfaces
   529→* Set route_localnet on eth0
   530→
   531→. NAT Configuration:
   532→* Add POSTROUTING on eth0:
   533→** Source from eth1 subnet
   534→** MASQUERADE to eth0 IP
   535→* Preserve rules marked "RBM-PORT-FORWARD"
   536→
   537→. DNS Server Access:
   538→* If
   539→{rbrr_dns_server}
   540→is configured:
   541→** Add rule to RBM-EGRESS accepting traffic to
   542→{rbrr_dns_server}
   543→** Add rule to RBM-FORWARD accepting traffic to
   544→{rbrr_dns_server}
   545→* These rules are independent of
   546→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   547→and
   548→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}
   549→
   550→. Filter Configuration:
   551→* If
   552→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode} is global:
   553→** Accept all output on eth0 in RBM-EGRESS
   554→** Accept all forward from eth1 in RBM-FORWARD
   555→* If
   556→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode} is allowlist:
   557→** For each CIDR in
   558→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}:
   559→*** Accept in RBM-EGRESS matching destination
   560→*** Accept in RBM-FORWARD matching destination
   561→* Maintain existing port rules
   562→
   563→==== Phase 4: {scr_dns_step}
   564→
   565→If
   566→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   567→is disabled, the
   568→{at_sentry_container}:
   569→
   570→* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from eth1
   571→* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-EGRESS to eth0
   572→* Removes any existing DNS-related NAT rules
   573→
   574→If
   575→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   576→is global or allowlist, the
   577→{at_sentry_container}
   578→executes this sequence:
   579→
   580→. DNS Server Validation:
   581→+
   582→--
   583→* Verify
   584→{rbrr_dns_server}
   585→if specified:
   586→** TCP connect test to port 53 with 5 second timeout
   587→** UDP query for "." record with 6 second timeout
   588→* Fail if either test unsuccessful
   589→--
   590→
   591→. Dnsmasq Configuration:
   592→+
   593→--
   594→* Core Settings:
   595→** Interface binding limited strictly to
   596→{at_enclave_network}
   597→interface eth1
   598→** Use {rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
   599→for listen-address
   600→** DNS service only - all DHCP functionality explicitly disabled
   601→** DNS resolution configuration isolated from host system settings
   602→** Clear existing upstream server configuration
   603→** Configure upstream DNS:
   604→*** Use configured upstream server when
   605→{rbrr_dns_server}
   606→specified
   607→*** Otherwise use host resolver via external network interface
   608→
   609→* Cache Settings:
   610→** Cache size: 1000 entries
   611→** Minimum TTL: 600 seconds
   612→** Maximum TTL: 3600 seconds
   613→
   614→* Query Control:
   615→** When
   616→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   617→is global:
   618→*** Accept all DNS queries from eth1
   619→*** Forward queries unmodified to upstream
   620→*** Log each query with timestamp and resolution
   621→*** Return all responses unmodified
   622→
   623→** When
   624→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   625→is allowlist:
   626→*** Load allowed domains from
   627→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}
   628→*** Accept queries only for allowed domains
   629→*** Return NXDOMAIN for all other queries
   630→*** Configure dnsmasq logging directly with:
   631→**** log-queries=extra
   632→**** log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log
   633→**** log-dhcp
   634→**** Extra logging options in dnsmasq.conf:
   635→***** log-debug (for detailed query info)
   636→***** log-async=20 (for better performance)
   637→--
   638→
   639→. Filter Rules:
   640→+
   641→--
   642→* Accept DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from eth1:
   643→** UDP port 53
   644→** TCP port 53
   645→* Accept DNS forwarding in RBM-EGRESS to eth0:
   646→** UDP/TCP port 53 to
   647→{rbrr_dns_server}
   648→** Or to host resolver if no server specified
   649→--
   650→
   651→. NAT Configuration:
   652→+
   653→--
   654→* DNAT eth1 DNS to
   655→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}:53
   656→* SNAT outbound queries to eth0 IP
   657→* Preserve existing port forwarding rules
   658→--

   718→== VM Management Operations
   719→
   720→This section defines the operations for managing Podman VM images within the {at_rbm_system}.
   721→
   722→=== {ops_rbv_check}
   723→
   724→The {ops_rbv_check} verifies whether newer VM images are available upstream by comparing SHA digests. This operation uses a temporary {at_stash_machine} with crane installed to query registries without modifying configuration.
   725→
   726→==== {ops_rbv_check} Sequence
   727→
   728→. Create Temporary {at_stash_machine}
   729→* Initialize new Podman machine using name from
   730→{rbrr_stash_machine}
   731→* Start the machine and install crane from
   732→{rbrr_crane_tar_gz}
   733→* Verify crane installation completed successfully
   734→
   735→. Query Upstream Image
   736→* Use crane to query digest of
   737→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_origin}:{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}
   738→* Store the returned SHA digest for comparison
   739→* Fail if query returns no valid digest
   740→
   741→. Verify Custom FQIN (if applicable)
   742→* If
   743→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin}
   744→differs from standard origin:version pattern:
   745→** Query the FQIN directly using crane
   746→** Compare FQIN digest against upstream digest
   747→** Report any discrepancies as warnings
   748→
   749→. Cleanup Resources
   750→* Stop and remove the temporary
   751→{at_stash_machine}
   752→* Ensure no orphaned resources remain
   753→
   754→. Report Findings
   755→* Display current
   756→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   757→value
   758→* Display latest upstream SHA digest
   759→* Indicate required actions:
   760→** If SHA not set: request user to set
   761→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   762→** If SHA differs: notify update available
   763→** If SHA matches: confirm system is current
   764→
   765→=== {ops_rbv_mirror}
   766→
   767→The
   768→{ops_rbv_mirror}
   769→copies the chosen VM image from upstream to the user's GHCR, ensuring consistent deployment across environments.
   770→This operation validates preconditions, performs the copy, and verifies podman can access the mirrored image.
   771→
   772→==== {ops_rbv_mirror} Sequence
   773→
   774→. Validate Preconditions
   775→* If using standard origin (FQIN starts with origin):
   776→** Query current upstream SHA using crane
   777→** Compare with
   778→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   779→if defined
   780→** Fail if SHAs do not match, directing user to run
   781→{ops_rbv_check}
   782→first
   783→
   784→. Generate GHCR Target
   785→* Construct GHCR tag using registry naming pattern
   786→* Format:
   787→ghcr.io/{rbrr_registry_owner}/{rbrr_registry_name}/vmimage:{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}
   788→
   789→. Check Existing Mirror
   790→* Query GHCR for existing image at target location
   791→* If found and SHA defined:
   792→** Verify existing image SHA matches
   793→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   794→** Exit successfully if match confirmed
   795→** Fail if SHAs differ
   796→
   797→. Copy Image to GHCR
   798→* Use crane to copy from
   799→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin}
   800→to GHCR target
   801→* Verify copy completed without errors
   802→* Confirm transferred image integrity
   803→
   804→. Verify Copied Image
   805→* If
   806→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   807→is defined:
   808→** Query digest of newly copied GHCR image
   809→** Compare with expected SHA
   810→** Fail if verification shows mismatch
   811→
   812→. Validate Podman Access
   813→* Initialize test Podman machine using mirrored image
   814→* Capture initialization output through standard scrubbing process
   815→* Extract reported SHA from initialization
   816→* Compare with
   817→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   818→if defined
   819→* Remove test machine after validation
   820→
   821→. Report Success
   822→* Confirm mirror operation completed
   823→* Note image available at GHCR location
   824→* Verify podman can successfully use mirrored image
   825→

   857→== {cmk_script} Requirements
   858→
   859→The following requirements apply to all {cmk_script_s} within the {cmk_discipline}:
   860→
   861→=== Environment Variable Availability
   862→
   863→Each
   864→{at_startup_script}
   865→inherits and validates
   866→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
   867→through makefile export directives:
   868→
   869→. At script start, validate existence of all referenced variables using `: ${VARIABLE:?} && echo "VARIABLE = ${VARIABLE}"`
   870→. {cfg_base_regime}, {cfg_station_regime}, and {cfg_nameplate_regime}
   871→export their respective variables
   872→
   873→=== Environment and Structure Requirements
   874→- Each
   875→{cmk_script}
   876→begins with `#!/bin/sh`
   877→- Each
   878→{cmk_script}
   879→enables `set -e`
   880→- Each
   881→{cmk_script}
   882→validates required environment variables using `: ${VARIABLE:?}`
   883→- Each
   884→{cmk_script}
   885→echoes validated variable values
   886→
   887→=== Error Handling Requirements 
   888→- Every command that can fail is followed by `|| exit N` where N is a positive integer
   889→- Error handling applies within if/then/else blocks and loops
   890→- Common commands like echo use exit code 99 for failures
   891→* Exit Code Ranges and Meanings:
   892→** 10: IPTables failures
   893→** 20: Port forwarding configuration failures
   894→** 30: Network access configuration failures 
   895→** 31: Network routing/filtering setup failures
   896→** 32: CIDR rule configuration failures
   897→** 40: DNS server connectivity check failures
   898→** 41: DNS configuration file writing failures
   899→** 42: DNS service startup failures
   900→** 43: DNS rule configuration failures
   901→
   902→=== Control Flow Requirements
   903→- if/then/else statements are permitted for clear flow control
   904→- for/while loops are permitted for iteration
   905→- All commands within control structures maintain error handling
   906→- Break and continue are permitted within loops
   907→- No command substitution without error handling
   908→- No function definitions permitted
   909→- All logic is expressed through direct command sequences
   910→
   911→=== Operation Logging Requirements
   912→- Each echo uses double quotes
   913→- Each log line begins with "RBSpN: " where N is the phase number (1-5)
   914→- Echo statements replace inline comments
   915→- No shell comments (# or multi-line) permitted
   916→- Debug echo statements precede significant operations
   917→
   918→=== Example Conformant Script Structure
   919→
   920→```sh
   921→#!/bin/sh
   922→set -e
   923→
   924→: "${INPUT_DIR:?}"
   925→: "${OUTPUT_DIR:?}"
   926→
   927→echo "XYZp1: Processing files from ${INPUT_DIR} to ${OUTPUT_DIR}"
   928→
   929→if [ ! -d "${INPUT_DIR}" ]; then
   930→    echo "XYZp1: Input directory not found"
   931→    exit 1
   932→fi
   933→
   934→if [ ! -d "${OUTPUT_DIR}" ]; then
   935→    echo "XYZp1: Creating output directory"
   936→    mkdir -p "${OUTPUT_DIR}" || exit 2
   937→fi
   938→
   939→echo "XYZp1: Beginning file conversion"
   940→for src in "${INPUT_DIR}"/*.txt; do
   941→    if [ -f "${src}" ]; then
   942→        echo "XYZp1: Processing ${src}"
   943→        dest="${OUTPUT_DIR}/$(basename "${src}" .txt).upper"
   944→        tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]' < "${src}" > "${dest}" || exit 3
   945→        wc -l "${dest}" > "${dest}.count" || exit 4
   946→    fi
   947→done
   948→
   949→echo "XYZp1: Processing complete"
   950→```

   952→== {cfg_base_regime} Definitions

   954→This section defines the

   961→[[rbrr_dns_server]]
   962→{rbrr_dns_server}:: 
   963→An IPv4 address (e.g., "8.8.8.8") that specifies the upstream DNS resolver for all 
   964→{at_bottle_service_s}.
   965→When configured, the
   966→{at_sentry_container}
   967→forwards DNS queries to this address instead of the host resolver, enabling consistent name resolution across all
   968→{at_bottle_container_s}
   969→while maintaining the established security boundaries.
   970→If not specified, the system uses the host's resolver configuration.
   971→
   972→[[rbrr_nameplate_path]]
   973→{rbrr_nameplate_path}:: 
   974→A relative path (e.g., "recipes/nameplates") that specifies the directory containing all
   975→{at_nameplate}
   976→definitions for this
   977→{at_rbm_system}.
   978→This path is resolved relative to the
   979→{at_rbm_repo}
   980→root, enabling consistent access to configuration files across different installations.
   981→If not specified, the system uses "nameplates" in the repository root.
   982→
   983→[[rbrr_registry_server]]

   986→{at_bottle_service_s}.
   987→The
   988→{at_rbm_system}
   989→retrieves all
   990→{st_published_image_s}
   991→from this registry, ensuring consistent image sourcing across the installation.
   992→If not specified, the system uses the default public container registry.
   993→
   994→[[rbrr_registry_owner]]

  1000→[[rbrr_registry_name]]

  1003→{rbrr_registry_owner}.

  1005→[[rbrr_github_pat_env]]

  1011→[[rbrr_build_architectures]]
  1012→{rbrr_build_architectures}::

  1020→{at_rbm_system}

  1055→== Architecture Term Definitions
  1056→
  1057→[[bottle_image]]
  1058→{at_bottle_image}::
  1059→A
  1060→{st_image}
  1061→that contains the service functionality intended to run within a
  1062→{at_bottle_container}.
  1063→This image is stored in the
  1064→{at_container_registry}
  1065→and is referenced by
  1066→{rbrn_bottle_repo_path} and {rbrn_bottle_image_tag}.
  1067→
  1068→[[bottle_service]]
  1069→{at_bottle_service}::
  1070→A complete service instance consisting of one
  1071→{at_sentry_container},
  1072→one
  1073→{at_censer_container},
  1074→and one
  1075→{at_bottle_container}
  1076→working together to provide secure functionality. Each service maintains strict file system and network boundaries while enabling controlled access to resources.
  1077→
  1078→[[sessile_service]]
  1079→{at_sessile_service}::
  1080→A
  1081→{at_bottle_service}
  1082→that maintains a persistent
  1083→{at_bottle_container}
  1084→running continuously to provide long-term services like development tools or network services.
  1085→These services maintain state and provide stable network ports while enforcing all
  1086→{at_sentry_container}
  1087→security constraints.
  1088→
  1089→[[agile_service]]
  1090→{at_agile_service}::
  1091→A
  1092→{at_bottle_service}
  1093→that creates temporary
  1094→{at_bottle_container_s}
  1095→for single operations, immediately removing them upon completion. This pattern suits toolchain operations that primarily operate through
  1096→{st_volume_mount_s}
  1097→with minimal network requirements.
  1098→
  1099→[[workstation]]
  1100→{at_workstation}::
  1101→The local computing environment where the
  1102→{rbtr_consumer}
  1103→runs the
  1104→{at_rbm_system}.
  1105→This environment hosts all
  1106→{at_bottle_service_s}
  1107→and their associated networks while providing isolation from other system resources.
  1108→
  1109→[[enclave_network]]
  1110→{at_enclave_network}::
  1111→An isolated network connecting a
  1112→{at_bottle_container}
  1113→to its
  1114→{at_sentry_container},
  1115→using the
  1116→// TODO involve the new network namespace
  1117→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
  1118→for addressing.
  1119→This network enforces strict security policies and provides the only network communication path for the
  1120→{at_bottle_container}.
  1121→
  1122→[[transit_network]]
  1123→{at_transit_network}::
  1124→The network interface that connects a
  1125→{at_sentry_container}
  1126→to the
  1127→{at_workstation_p}
  1128→network resources.
  1129→This network enables controlled external communication while maintaining security boundaries.
  1130→
  1131→[[moniker]]
  1132→{at_moniker}::
  1133→A unique identifier specified by
  1134→{rbrn_moniker}
  1135→that distinguishes each
  1136→{at_bottle_service}
  1137→instance within the
  1138→{at_rbm_system}.
  1139→This identifier forms the basis for container names and network identifiers.
  1140→
  1141→[[nameplate]]
  1142→{at_nameplate}::
  1143→A configuration file stored in
  1144→{rbrr_nameplate_path}
  1145→that defines all aspects of a
  1146→{at_bottle_service},
  1147→including its images, network settings, and security policies.
  1148→Each nameplate provides a complete service specification that the
  1149→{at_rbm_system}
  1150→can instantiate.
  1151→
  1152→[[transit_network_name]]
  1153→{at_transit_network_name}::
  1154→The unique network name formed by appending `-uplink` to the service's
  1155→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1156→The
  1157→{at_rbm_system}
  1158→uses this consistent naming pattern
  1159→`${rbrn_moniker}-uplink`
  1160→to identify the external-facing network for each
  1161→{at_sentry_container}
  1162→within the container runtime environment.
  1163→
  1164→[[enclave_network_name]]
  1165→{at_enclave_network_name}::
  1166→The unique network name formed by appending `-enclave` to the service's
  1167→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1168→The
  1169→{at_rbm_system}
  1170→uses this consistent naming pattern `${rbrn_moniker}-enclave` to identify the isolated internal network connecting each
  1171→{at_bottle_container}
  1172→to its
  1173→{at_sentry_container}.
  1174→
  1175→[[enclave_namespace_name]]
  1176→{at_enclave_namespace_name}::
  1177→The
  1178→// TODO enclave, transit, network, but then namespace: this all needs a term rebalance... but later
  1179→{at_bottle_service}
  1180→unique namespace name formed by appending `-namespace` to the service's
  1181→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1182→// TODO this definition is not precise, but I'm not currently smart enough to repair it
  1183→The
  1184→{at_rbm_system}
  1185→uses this consistent naming pattern `${rbrn_moniker}-namespace` to identify the isolated internal network namespace connecting each
  1186→{at_bottle_container}
  1187→to its
  1188→{at_sentry_container}.
  1189→
  1190→[[sentry_image]]
  1191→{at_sentry_image}::
  1192→A
  1193→{st_image}
  1194→that contains the security enforcement functionality for a
  1195→{at_sentry_container}.
  1196→This image is stored in the
  1197→{at_container_registry}
  1198→and is referenced by
  1199→{rbrn_sentry_repo_path} and {rbrn_sentry_image_tag}.
  1200→
  1201→[[sentry_container]]
  1202→{at_sentry_container}::
  1203→A privileged container that enforces network security policies for a
  1204→{at_bottle_service}.
  1205→It connects to both
  1206→{at_transit_network} and {at_enclave_network},
  1207→controlling all network traffic and providing DNS services.
  1208→
  1209→[[censer_container]]
  1210→{at_censer_container}::
  1211→A privileged container using a minimal image that establishes and configures the network namespace before the
  1212→{at_bottle_container}
  1213→starts.
  1214→It sets up routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
  1215→{at_sentry_container},
  1216→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
  1217→{at_bottle_container}
  1218→via `--net=container:censer`.
  1219→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet.
  1220→
  1221→[[bottle_container]]
  1222→{at_bottle_container}::
  1223→A container that runs the actual service functionality, inheriting its network namespace from the
  1224→{at_censer_container}.
  1225→It is connected only to the
  1226→{at_enclave_network}
  1227→and configured with specific
  1228→{st_volume_mount_s}.
  1229→All external communication flows through its associated
  1230→{at_sentry_container}.
  1231→
  1232→[[bottle_container_name]]
  1233→{at_bottle_container_name}::
  1234→The 
  1235→{at_bottle_container}
  1236→formed by appending `-bottle` to the service's
  1237→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1238→The
  1239→{at_rbm_system}
  1240→uses this consistent naming pattern
  1241→`${rbrn_moniker}-bottle`
  1242→to identify all
  1243→{at_bottle_container_s}
  1244→within the container runtime environment, enabling reliable service management and status tracking.
  1245→
  1246→[[sentry_container_name]]
  1247→{at_sentry_container_name}::
  1248→The unique container name formed by appending `-sentry` to the service's
  1249→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1250→The
  1251→{at_rbm_system}
  1252→uses this consistent naming pattern
  1253→`${rbrn_moniker}-sentry`
  1254→to identify all
  1255→{at_sentry_container_s}
  1256→within the container runtime environment, enabling reliable security policy enforcement and network management.
  1257→
  1258→[[rbm_console]]
  1259→{at_rbm_console}::
  1260→The primary
  1261→{cmk_makefile}
  1262→that implements the
  1263→{at_rbm_system_p}
  1264→operational rules. It provides
  1265→{cmk_external_rule_s} and {cmk_internal_rule_s}
  1266→for managing
  1267→{at_bottle_service_s}
  1268→throughout their lifecycle.
  1269→
  1270→[[rbm_config_makefile]]
  1271→{at_rbm_config_makefile}::
  1272→A
  1273→{cmk_makefile}
  1274→that manages configuration settings for the
  1275→{at_rbm_system},
  1276→including
  1277→{at_rbm_secret_s}
  1278→and environment-specific parameters.
  1279→This file typically remains outside version control to protect sensitive data.
  1280→
  1281→[[rbm_system]]
  1282→{at_rbm_system}::
  1283→The complete Recipe Bottle Makefile system, consisting of the
  1284→{at_rbm_console}, {at_rbm_config_makefile}, {at_nameplate_s},
  1285→and associated tools.
  1286→This system enables secure deployment and management of containerized services.
  1287→
  1288→[[rbm_secret]]
  1289→{at_rbm_secret}::
  1290→A sensitive configuration value needed by the
  1291→{at_rbm_system},
  1292→such as
  1293→registry credentials.
  1294→These secrets are managed through the
  1295→{at_rbm_config_makefile}
  1296→and kept separate from version control.
  1297→
  1298→[[rbm_repo]]
  1299→{at_rbm_repo}::
  1300→The version control repository containing the
  1301→{at_rbm_system}
  1302→implementation, including the
  1303→{at_rbm_console}, {at_nameplate_s},
  1304→and documentation.
  1305→This repository serves as the authoritative source for system configuration.
  1306→
  1307→[[user_repo]]
  1308→{at_user_repo}::
  1309→A version control repository owned by a
  1310→{rbtr_consumer}.
  1311→The
  1312→{rbtr_consumer}
  1313→chooses how to instantiate the
  1314→{at_rbm_system}
  1315→there, though a git subtree is a good choice.
  1316→The
  1317→{rbtr_consumer}
  1318→configures the
  1319→{at_rbm_system}
  1320→by committing a
  1321→{cfg_base_regime}
  1322→makefile and then creates a set of
  1323→{at_nameplate_s}
  1324→compliant with the
  1325→{cfg_nameplate_regime}
  1326→to define
  1327→{at_bottle_service_s}.
  1328→
  1329→[[container_registry]]
  1330→{at_container_registry}:: 
  1331→A service that stores and provides access to 
  1332→{st_published_image_s}.
  1333→
  1334→[[startup_script]]
  1335→{at_startup_script}::
  1336→A
  1337→{cmk_script}
  1338→that performs initialization and configuration tasks when starting a
  1339→{at_bottle_service}.
  1340→These scripts follow strict requirements for execution environment, logging, and error handling.
  1341→
  1342→[[consumer]]
  1343→{rbtr_consumer}::
  1344→An individual operating the
  1345→{at_rbm_system}
  1346→to run containerized services securely on their
  1347→{at_workstation}.
  1348→{rbtr_consumer_s}
  1349→control service configuration through
  1350→{at_nameplate_s}
  1351→and interact with services through controlled interfaces, distinct from infrastructure operators who build and deploy the system.
  1352→
  1353→[[at_ebpf_program]]
  1354→{at_ebpf_program}::
  1355→An eBPF TC (Traffic Control) filter program attached to the
  1356→{at_bottle_container_p}
  1357→veth interface that intercepts and rewrites Ethernet frames at the packet level.
  1358→This program replaces the default gateway IP address assigned by netavark with the
  1359→{at_sentry_container_p}
  1360→IP address in all outbound frames, ensuring all
  1361→{at_bottle_container}
  1362→network traffic routes through its associated
  1363→{at_sentry_container}
  1364→without requiring network namespace manipulation or special container privileges.
  1365→
  1366→[[base_regime]]
  1367→{cfg_base_regime}::
  1368→A
  1369→{crg_regime}
  1370→that defines and enforces core system configuration through environment variables with the RBB_*
  1371→{crg_prefix}.
  1372→The
  1373→{at_rbm_repo}
  1374→contains exactly one Base Config makefile that exports these variables, establishing foundational configuration for a single
  1375→{at_rbm_system}
  1376→installation.
  1377→
  1378→[[station_regime]]
  1379→{cfg_station_regime}::
  1380→A
  1381→{crg_regime}
  1382→that defines and enforces deployment-specific configuration through environment variables with the RBS_*
  1383→{crg_prefix}.
  1384→Each
  1385→{rbtr_consumer}
  1386→maintains their own Station Config makefile outside version control, containing sensitive data like authentication tokens required for their specific installation.
  1387→
  1388→[[nameplate_regime]]
  1389→{cfg_nameplate_regime}::
  1390→A
  1391→{crg_regime}
  1392→that defines and enforces service configuration through environment variables with the RBRN_*
  1393→{crg_prefix}.
  1394→Each
  1395→{at_bottle_service}
  1396→has exactly one
  1397→{at_nameplate}
  1398→makefile that exports these variables, establishing that service's precise behavior and constraints.
  1399→
  1400→[[regime_variable]]
  1401→{cfg_regime_variable}::
  1402→A
  1403→{crg_variable}
  1404→exported by one of the three configuration regime makefiles, following that regime's
  1405→{crg_prefix}
  1406→(RBB_*, RBS_*, or RBRN_*).
  1407→Each
  1408→{at_startup_script}
  1409→validates required
  1410→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
  1411→using the ': ${VARIABLE:?}' pattern before use.
  1412→
  1413→[[stash_machine]]
  1414→{at_stash_machine}::
  1415→A temporary Podman virtual machine created with crane installed for performing registry queries and image operations.
  1416→These machines are removed after completing their specific tasks.
  1417→
  1418→[[operational_machine]]
  1419→{at_operational_machine}::
  1420→The persistent Podman virtual machine that hosts all bottle services and their associated containers in the RBM system.
  1421→
  1422→== {cfg_station_regime} Definitions
  1423→
  1424→This section defines the
  1425→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
  1426→which come from the
  1427→{cfg_station_regime}
  1428→that defines user specific configuration for the
  1429→{at_rbm_system}.
  1430→
  1431→This
  1432→{crg_regime}
  1433→provides settings that are specific to a particular local {at_rbm_repo} clone or checkout.
  1434→These settings typically contain sensitive data like authentication tokens that should not be committed to version control.
  1435→
  1436→== Nameplate Configuration Regime Definitions
  1437→
  1438→This section defines the
  1439→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
  1440→which come from the
  1441→{cfg_nameplate_regime}.
  1442→Each
  1443→{at_bottle_service}
  1444→has exactly one
  1445→{at_nameplate}
  1446→makefile that exports these variables, establishing that service's precise behavior and constraints.
  1447→
  1448→[[rbrn_moniker]]
  1449→{rbrn_moniker}::
  1450→A unique identifier that distinguishes this
  1451→{at_bottle_service}
  1452→from others in the
  1453→{at_rbm_system}.
  1454→
  1455→[[rbrn_description]]
  1456→{rbrn_description}::
  1457→A human-readable explanation of the
  1458→{at_bottle_service_p}
  1459→purpose and function.
  1460→
  1461→[[rbrn_sentry_moniker]]
  1462→{rbrn_sentry_moniker}::
  1463→The image moniker identifier for the
  1464→{at_sentry_image}
  1465→in the
  1466→{at_container_registry}.
  1467→
  1468→[[rbrn_bottle_moniker]]
  1469→{rbrn_bottle_moniker}::
  1470→The image moniker identifier for the
  1471→{at_bottle_image}
  1472→in the
  1473→{at_container_registry}.
  1474→
  1475→[[rbrn_sentry_image_tag]]
  1476→{rbrn_sentry_image_tag}::
  1477→The version identifier for the
  1478→{at_sentry_image}
  1479→to be deployed.
  1480→
  1481→[[rbrn_bottle_image_tag]]
  1482→{rbrn_bottle_image_tag}::
  1483→The version identifier for the
  1484→{at_bottle_image}
  1485→to be deployed.
  1486→
  1487→[[rbrn_entry_mode]]
  1488→{rbrn_entry_mode}::
  1489→The mode controlling whether the
  1490→{at_bottle_service}
  1491→exposes network ports through the
  1492→{at_sentry_container}.
  1493→When disabled, no entry ports are configured.
  1494→When enabled, entry ports are mapped through the
  1495→{at_sentry_container}.
  1496→
  1497→[[rbrn_entry_port_workstation]]
  1498→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}::
  1499→The network port number exposed on the
  1500→{at_transit_network}
  1501→when port service is enabled.
  1502→
  1503→[[rbrn_entry_port_enclave]]
  1504→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}::
  1505→The network port number used for communication between
  1506→{at_sentry_container}
  1507→and
  1508→{at_bottle_container}
  1509→across the
  1510→{at_enclave_network}.
  1511→
  1512→[[rbrn_uplink_dns_mode]]
  1513→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}::
  1514→The DNS resolution mode for bottle-initiated requests through the
  1515→{at_sentry_container}.
  1516→When disabled, the
  1517→{at_sentry_container} blocks all DNS queries from the
  1518→{at_bottle_container}.
  1519→When global, the
  1520→{at_sentry_container}
  1521→forwards all DNS queries unmodified to upstream.
  1522→When allowlist, the
  1523→{at_sentry_container}
  1524→allows resolution only for domains listed in
  1525→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}
  1526→and returns NXDOMAIN for all other queries.
  1527→
  1528→[[rbrn_uplink_access_mode]]
  1529→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}::
  1530→The IP access mode for bottle-initiated connections through the
  1531→{at_transit_network}.
  1532→When disabled, all non-port traffic is blocked.
  1533→When global, unrestricted IP access is allowed.
  1534→When allowlist, only traffic to CIDRs listed in
  1535→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}
  1536→is permitted.
  1537→
  1538→[[rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs]]
  1539→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}::
  1540→A space-separated list of CIDR ranges that define allowed outbound traffic through the
  1541→{at_transit_network}.
  1542→
  1543→[[rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains]]
  1544→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}::
  1545→A space-separated list of domain names that are allowed for DNS resolution through the
  1546→{at_sentry_container}.
  1547→
  1548→[[rbrn_enclave_netmask]]
  1549→{rbrn_enclave_netmask}::
  1550→The network mask width defining the size of address space available for the
  1551→{at_enclave_network}.
  1552→
  1553→[[rbrn_enclave_base_ip]]
  1554→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}::
  1555→The base IPv4 address that establishes the starting point of the
  1556→{at_enclave_network}
  1557→range.
  1558→
  1559→[[rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip]]
  1560→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}::
  1561→The permanent gateway IP address (typically .0.1) assigned to the
  1562→{at_sentry_container}
  1563→on the
  1564→{at_enclave_network}
  1565→after removing the initial auto-assigned address. This address serves as the gateway for all
  1566→{at_bottle_container_s}
  1567→on the
  1568→{at_enclave_network}.
  1569→
  1570→[[rbrn_volume_mounts]]
  1571→{rbrn_volume_mounts}::
  1572→A space-separated list of Podman volume mount arguments that define filesystem access for the
  1573→{at_bottle_container}.
  1574→
  1575→== Console Makefile Elements
  1576→
  1577→[[cmk_console_discipline]]
  1578→{cmk_discipline}::
  1579→A systematic approach to automation that defines patterns for creating reliable, maintainable build systems through careful rule organization, strict error handling, and standardized operational sequencing. This discipline establishes conventions for multi-pass construction, naming patterns, and the relationships between makefiles, scripts, and configuration files.
  1580→
  1581→[[cmk_makefile]]
  1582→{cmk_makefile}::
  1583→A specific makefile implementation that follows the
  1584→{cmk_discipline},
  1585→expressing concrete automation through properly structured
  1586→{cmk_external_rule_s}, {cmk_internal_rule_s}, and {cmk_script_s}
  1587→while maintaining prescribed error handling and operational patterns.
  1588→
  1589→[[cmk_clause]]
  1590→{cmk_clause}::
  1591→A standardized sequence of commands or expressions within a
  1592→{cmk_makefile}
  1593→that provides consistent behavior across different rules and contexts while maintaining
  1594→{cmk_discipline}
  1595→patterns.
  1596→These clauses can be invoked directly, used with $(call), or incorporated into larger recipes.
  1597→
  1598→[[cmk_recipe_line]]
  1599→{cmk_recipe_line}::
  1600→A single command line within a
  1601→{cmk_external_rule} or {cmk_internal_rule}
  1602→that follows
  1603→{cmk_discipline}
  1604→conventions, expressing one concrete bash action.
  1605→
  1606→[[cmk_probe_line]]
  1607→{cmk_probe_line}::
  1608→A single makefile recipe line that executes a precise command within a container context.
  1609→Recipe lines form the verification backbone of the
  1610→{cmk_discipline},
  1611→ensuring proper state progression through operational sequences.
  1612→
  1613→[[cmk_internal_rule]]
  1614→{cmk_internal_rule}::
  1615→A makefile rule that coordinates implementation details within the
  1616→{cmk_discipline},
  1617→called by other
  1618→{cmk_external_rule_s} or {cmk_internal_rule_s}
  1619→to perform specific operational sequences.
  1620→
  1621→[[cmk_external_rule]]
  1622→{cmk_external_rule}::
  1623→A makefile rule that provides primary interface points for
  1624→{rbtr_consumer}
  1625→use, typically invoked by shell scripts or direct user commands to initiate major system operations.
  1626→
  1627→[[cmk_script]]
  1628→{cmk_script}::
  1629→A bash script that exists as an integral part of a
  1630→{cmk_discipline}
  1631→installation, executed under control of
  1632→{cmk_external_rule_s} or {cmk_internal_rule_s}
  1633→and sharing the makefile's operational context and constraints.
  1634→
  1635→[[cmk_script_line]]
  1636→{cmk_script_line}::
  1637→One or more command lines within a
  1638→{cmk_script}
  1639→that achieve a particular action in the script's operational sequence, following
  1640→{cmk_discipline}
  1641→conventions for error handling and status reporting.
  1642→Any failure in a script line results in a non-zero exit status.
  1643→
  1644→== Supporting Infrastructure Definitions
  1645→
  1646→[[container]]
  1647→{st_container}:: 
  1648→A lightweight, standalone executable package containing all necessary components to run a service.
  1649→
  1650→[[dockerfile]]
  1651→{st_dockerfile}:: 
  1652→A specification file that defines how to build a 
  1653→{st_image}.
  1654→
  1655→[[image]] 
  1656→{st_image}:: 
  1657→A template used to create 
  1658→{st_container}
